name: Algerian Saharan Margin

staple_foods.name: Sigoise olive

primary_metabolites.oleic_acid: 59-62% of total fatty acids [Sigoise, Eloued — Guerfel et al. 2022 Food Sci Nutr]
primary_metabolites.linoleic_acid: 15-17% of total fatty acids [Guerfel et al. 2022]

key_flavor_bioactives.polyphenol_content: 838-945 ppm total polyphenols [Sigoise olive, Eloued water-stressed — Guerfel et al. 2022 Food Sci Nutr] — 60% higher than Setif highlands
key_flavor_bioactives.tocopherol_content: 208-245 mg/kg [Sigoise, Eloued — Guerfel et al. 2022] — 2.4x higher than Setif

staple_foods.notes: Saharan margin Eloued site. Severe water stress (65mm annual precipitation) drives dramatic upregulation of polyphenols (60% higher) and tocopherols (2.4x higher) vs. highland RSU-15 Setif site. Oleic acid proportion is lower and linoleic acid higher than highland counterpart — water stress shifts fatty acid composition. Natural experiment pair with RSU-15 (Algerian Northern Highlands) — same Sigoise cultivar under contrasting water availability.
data_sources: Guerfel et al. 2022 Food Sci Nutr PMC9179137 — Sigoise olive oil composition across Algerian regions

staple_foods.name: dates

primary_metabolites.sugar_content: ~65-70 g/100g total sugars [dates — literature estimate, FDC data needed]

key_flavor_bioactives.polyphenol_content: tannins and carotenoids present — FDC data needed for quantification

staple_foods.notes: Dates (Phoenix dactylifera) are the defining carbohydrate staple of Saharan oasis communities. Algeria's Eloued region (El Oued) is renowned for its Deglet Nour dates. Extremely high sugar concentration with low water content. FDC data needed for full metabolite profile.

staple_foods.name: camel meat

umami_compounds.glutamate: ~0.3 g/100g free glutamate [camelid muscle — literature estimate, FDC data needed]

staple_foods.notes: Camel (Camelus dromedarius) is the culturally significant protein source in Saharan nomadic and semi-nomadic food systems. Lean red meat with distinct fatty acid profile. FDC data limited — specialized literature required.

staple_foods.name: cumin

terpenes.cuminaldehyde: cuminaldehyde is the dominant volatile compound in cumin essential oil — 25-35% of oil fraction
terpenes.pinene: alpha- and beta-pinene present in cumin oil — secondary terpenes

staple_foods.notes: Cumin (Cuminum cyminum) is the dominant aromatic spice of Saharan and southern Algerian cuisine. Cuminaldehyde is the key flavor-active terpene. FDC data needed for macro profile.

staple_foods.name: smen

primary_metabolites.lipid_content: ~80 g/100g [clarified fermented butter — literature estimate, FDC data needed]

organic_acids.lactic_acid: present — LAB fermentation during smen production
organic_acids.butyric_acid: present — characteristic short-chain fatty acid of aged butter fermentation

staple_foods.notes: Smen is a traditional North African fermented and clarified butter, aged for months to years. The long fermentation produces a sharp, pungent flavor profile dominated by short-chain fatty acids including butyric acid. Used extensively in Saharan and Maghrebi cooking. FDC data not available — specialized literature required.

notes: Algerian Saharan margin site (Eloued region, ~70m elevation). Hyper-arid conditions (65mm annual precipitation) on aeolian sand parent material. Natural experiment pair with RSU-15 (Algerian Northern Highlands) — same Sigoise olive cultivar under contrasting water availability. Severe water stress drives the highest polyphenol and tocopherol concentrations measured in the Guerfel et al. 2022 dataset — a clean environmental terroir signal on identical genetic background. Oasis food system built around dates as primary carbohydrate energy source, with camel and goat as protein/fat providers adapted to arid conditions. Lowest agricultural intensification index in the pilot set.
